description: American International Group is a global insurance company focused primarily on commercial property-casualty coverage, specialty risk, and high-net-worth personal insurance.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: American International Group is one of the major global insurance groups, with a core emphasis on commercial property-casualty insurance for businesses, institutions, and specialized risk markets. Its offerings span areas such as casualty, financial lines, property, marine, aviation, multinational programs, and other forms of risk transfer that enterprises buy when optimism needs a deductible.

AIG also serves affluent individuals and families through Private Client Select, a personal insurance business oriented around homes, collections, automobiles, liability, and related high-value coverage needs.

overview.title: Strategic Context
paragraphs: AIG has spent recent years simplifying its corporate structure, sharpening underwriting discipline, and positioning itself more clearly around global commercial insurance. The company’s separation of its life and retirement business, Corebridge Financial, marked a major step in that reshaping.

For Free The World, AIG is best understood less as a consumer technology platform and more as a financial risk infrastructure company. Its public-interest footprint sits around pricing power, claims handling, access to insurance, risk modeling, broker distribution, and the opacity that can come with complex institutional insurance markets.

overview.title: Product Surface
paragraphs: The initial registry product surface should include AIG Commercial Insurance as the umbrella for its business and institutional coverage lines, plus AIG Private Client Select for high-net-worth personal insurance. Further structured sync should break commercial insurance into narrower lines only where AIG presents them as durable, customer-facing product families.

No technology waves are mapped yet in the repo context. Future research may identify relevant themes around insurance analytics, catastrophe modeling, cyber risk, digital claims workflows, and broker-facing platforms, but those should be added only after source-backed product evidence is collected.
